1	---
2	title: instrumentation.js
3	description: API reference for the instrumentation.js file.
4	related:
5	  title: Learn more about Instrumentation
6	  links:
7	    - app/guides/instrumentation
8	---
9	
10	The `instrumentation.js|ts` file is used to integrate observability tools into your application, allowing you to track the performance and behavior, and to debug issues in production.
11	
12	To use it, place the file in the **root** of your application or inside a [`src` folder](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/src-folder) if using one.
13	
14	## Exports
15	
16	### `register` (optional)
17	
18	The file exports a `register` function that is called **once** when a new Next.js server instance is initiated. `register` can be an async function.
19	
20	```ts filename="instrumentation.ts" switcher
21	import { registerOTel } from '@vercel/otel'
22	
23	export function register() {
24	  registerOTel('next-app')
25	}
26	```
27	
28	```js filename="instrumentation.js" switcher
29	import { registerOTel } from '@vercel/otel'
30	
31	export function register() {
32	  registerOTel('next-app')
33	}
34	```
35	
36	### `onRequestError` (optional)
37	
38	You can optionally export an `onRequestError` function to track **server** errors to any custom observability provider.
39	
40	- If you're running any async tasks in `onRequestError`, make sure they're awaited. `onRequestError` will be triggered when the Next.js server captures the error.
41	- The `error` instance might not be the original error instance thrown, as it may be processed by React if encountered during Server Components rendering. If this happens, you can use `digest` property on an error to identify the actual error type.
42	
43	```ts filename="instrumentation.ts" switcher
44	import { type Instrumentation } from 'next'
45	
46	export const onRequestError: Instrumentation.onRequestError = async (
47	  err,
48	  request,
49	  context
50	) => {
51	  await fetch('https://.../report-error', {
52	    method: 'POST',
53	    body: JSON.stringify({
54	      message: err.message,
55	      request,
56	      context,
57	    }),
58	    headers: {
59	      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
60	    },
61	  })
62	}
63	```
64	
65	```js filename="instrumentation.js" switcher
66	export async function onRequestError(err, request, context) {
67	  await fetch('https://.../report-error', {
68	    method: 'POST',
69	    body: JSON.stringify({
70	      message: err.message,
71	      request,
72	      context,
73	    }),
74	    headers: {
75	      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
76	    },
77	  })
78	}
79	```
80	
81	#### Parameters
82	
83	The function accepts three parameters: `error`, `request`, and `context`.
84	
85	```ts filename="Types"
86	export function onRequestError(
87	  error: { digest: string } & Error,
88	  request: {
89	    path: string // resource path, e.g. /blog?name=foo
90	    method: string // request method. e.g. GET, POST, etc
91	    headers: { [key: string]: string | string[] }
92	  },
93	  context: {
94	    routerKind: 'Pages Router' | 'App Router' // the router type
95	    routePath: string // the route file path, e.g. /app/blog/[dynamic]
96	    routeType: 'render' | 'route' | 'action' | 'proxy' // the context in which the error occurred
97	    renderSource:
98	      | 'react-server-components'
99	      | 'react-server-components-payload'
100	      | 'server-rendering'
101	    revalidateReason: 'on-demand' | 'stale' | undefined // undefined is a normal request without revalidation
102	    renderType: 'dynamic' | 'dynamic-resume' // 'dynamic-resume' for PPR
103	  }
104	): void | Promise<void>
105	```
106	
107	- `error`: The caught error itself (type is always `Error`), and a `digest` property which is the unique ID of the error.
108	- `request`: Read-only request information associated with the error.
109	- `context`: The context in which the error occurred. This can be the type of router (App or Pages Router), and/or (Server Components (`'render'`), Route Handlers (`'route…(+1141)
